hard_deny: Never autonomously run `fastboot flash <partition>` for any non-HLOS partition (bootloader, abl, xbl, aboot, boot, recovery, dtbo, vbmeta, modem, dsp, persist, devinfo, etc.). Only `system`, `vendor`, `product`, `system_ext`, `odm`, `userdata`, `cache`, `metadata` (with optional `_a`/`_b` suffix) are HLOS-safe. Non-HLOS flash failures can hard-brick the device. Test gbl-chainload EFIs via `fastboot stage` + `fastboot oem boot-efi` only — that path is a one-shot RAM load that survives a power cycle.

Never autonomously run `fastboot oem unlock`, `fastboot oem lock`, `fastboot flashing {unlock,lock,unlock_critical,lock_critical}`, `fastboot --set-active`, or `fastboot erase` of any non-HLOS partition. These are lock-state / slot / persistent-state transitions that require explicit user confirmation in a real shell.
All non-trivial repo changes must land via a PR against `main`. Never commit directly to `main`; never push to `main`. Iterative work stays a commit series on the same feature branch.
